title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: Google sets August date for next Pixel event
slides.explanation: Google is holding its next hardware launch event on August 12th in New York City. The presentation starts at 6PM ET, which is a bit later than usual for these announcements.

slides.headline: Claude Cowork arrives on web and mobile
slides.explanation: Anthropic is bringing its AI platform to web browsers and mobile devices for the first time. Max subscribers get access starting Tuesday, with other users following in the coming weeks.

slides.headline: Meta's new AI can add people to your photos
slides.explanation: Meta launched a new AI image model that can pull other Instagram users into AI-generated photos. It is currently rolling out across Meta's main apps.

slides.headline: Microsoft layoffs impact Bethesda and id Software
slides.explanation: Reports indicate significant job cuts at Bethesda and id Software. Some teams have seen up to half of their staff affected by these reductions.

slides.headline: A look at Meta's new image tool, Muse
slides.explanation: Meta has introduced Muse, an AI model designed for creating images. It has several practical uses, ranging from advertising to creative projects.

caption: Big changes are hitting the tech world today, from new AI tools to major hardware announcements. We have broken down the top five stories you need to know about. Swipe through to catch up on the latest news. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

title: Top 5 Geopolitics Headlines

slides.headline: US strikes Iran after tanker attacks
slides.explanation: The US military carried out strikes in Iran following attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz. Washington says this is about holding Iran accountable, while Tehran is threatening to retaliate.

slides.headline: Le Pen to run for French presidency
slides.explanation: Marine Le Pen has confirmed she will run for president despite a recent court conviction. She plans to appeal the ruling, which allows her to campaign while wearing an electronic tag.

slides.headline: Explosions reported across Iran
slides.explanation: US forces have bombed targets in Sirik, Qeshm, and Bandar Abbas. These attacks come alongside new US restrictions on Iranian oil exports.

slides.headline: Trump revives plan to buy Greenland
slides.explanation: Donald Trump is again suggesting the US should acquire Greenland as he heads to a NATO summit. He also warned he might pull American troops out of Europe due to disagreements over Iran.

slides.headline: Canada sues OpenAI over shooting case
slides.explanation: A Canadian province is taking OpenAI to court. The lawsuit claims the company failed to warn authorities about violent messages generated by ChatGPT linked to a shooting suspect.

caption: From rising tensions in the Middle East to legal battles over AI technology, here is your quick update on today's top global stories. We break down what you need to know without the noise. Swipe through to catch up on the headlines. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
